Additionally, every argument in the MethodInvocation, if it is of type SingleVariableAccess, must correspond to an existing instance of SingleVariableAccess.

ocl: context ImportDeclaration inv: NamedElement.allInstances()->exists(ne | ne = self.importedElement and ne.originalCompilationUnit = self.originalCompilationUnit )
specification: For any instance of ImportDeclaration, there must exist a NamedElement which is both the imported element of that Importdeclaration and associated with the same original compilation unit.

ocl: context NamedElement inv: self.name <> 'var' and self.name <> '_' and self.name <> 'strictfp' and self.name <> 'assert' and self.name <> 'enum'
specification: The name of a NamedElement should not be 'var', '_', 'strictfp', 'assert', or 'enum'.
The name of a type declaration should start with an uppercase letter.

ocl: context MethodInvocation inv: self.method <> null implies not ( self.method.name = 'finalize' and self.method.parameters->isEmpty() )
specification: For a method invocation in the given context, if the method is not null, then its name should not be 'finalize' without any parameters.

ocl: context MethodInvocation inv: self.method <> null implies not ( self.method.name = 'exit' and self.method.parameters->size() = 1 and self.method.parameters->first().type.type.oclIsTypeOf(PrimitiveTypeInt) )
specification: For a method invocation, if the method is not null, it must not be the case that the method's name is 'exit' with exactly one parameter of integer primitive type.

ocl: context MethodInvocation inv: self.method <> null implies not ( self.method.name = 'runFinalizersOnExit' and self.method.parameters->size() = 1 and self.method.parameters->first().type.type.oclIsTypeOf(PrimitiveTypeBoolean) )
specification: If a method is not null in a method invocation, then it should not call the method named 'runFinalizersOnExit' with one boolean parameter.

ocl: context AbstractMethodDeclaration inv: (self._'body' <> null and self.parameters->notEmpty()) implies ( self._'body'.statements -> selectByKind(ExpressionStatement) -> collect(expression) -> selectByKind(Assignment) -> select(assignment | assignment.leftHandSide.oclIsKindOf(SingleVariableAccess) and assignment.operator = AssignmentKind::ASSIGN and assignment.leftHandSide.oclIsKindOf(SingleVariableAccess) and self.parameters->excludes(assignment.leftHandSide.oclAsType(SingleVariableAccess).variable) ) -> isEmpty() )
specification: If an abstract method declaration has a non-null body and contains parameters, then for every assignment statement within the body, the left-hand side of the assignment, which is a single variable access, must not be a parameter of the method.

ocl: context Assignment inv: self.operator = AssignmentKind::ASSIGN implies self.leftHandSide <> self.rightHandSide
specification: In an assignment, if the type of operator is 'ASSIGN', then the left-hand side must be different from the right-hand side.

ocl: context VariableDeclarationStatement inv: self.modifier.visibility = VisibilityKind::public implies self.modifier.inheritance = InheritanceKind::final
specification: A variable declaration statement where the visibility modifier is set to 'public' must also have its inheritance modifier set to 'final'.

ocl: context VariableDeclarationExpression inv: self.modifier.visibility = VisibilityKind::public implies self.modifier.inheritance = InheritanceKind::final
specification: If the visibility of a variable declaration is public, then its inheritance must be set to final.
In the context of a CatchClause, the exception must be used at least once.
The type of exception caught by a catch clause should not be named 'IllegalMonitorStateException'.

ocl: context Block inv: self.statements->isEmpty() implies self.comments->notEmpty()
specification: If a block contains no statements, it must have comments.
A switch statement should have more than three cases.
A superconstructor invocation must have at least one argument.
In an IfStatement, the expression should not be of the kind 'TypeLiteral'.

ocl: context IfStatement inv: not self.thenStatement.oclIsTypeOf(EmptyStatement) and (self.elseStatement <> null implies not self.elseStatement.oclIsTypeOf(EmptyStatement))

ocl: context User inv: self.Gender = 'male' or self.Gender = 'female'

The maximum number of roles in a session should be greater than or equal to the number of activated roles in that session.

ocl: context Object inv: Object.allInstances()->forAll(o1, o2 : Object | o1.ObjID = o2.ObjID implies o1 = o2)
specification: Each object must have a unique identification (ObjID), such that no two different objects share the same ObjID.

ocl: context map inv: roads->forAll(n1, n2 | n1.name <> n2.name)
specification: All roads on a map must have unique names.

ocl: context map inv: spaces->forAll(n1, n2 | n1.name <> n2.name)
specification: All spaces within a map should have unique names.

ocl: context map inv: roads->forAll(n1, n2 | n1.length <> n2.length)
specification: All roads on the map must have unique lengths.

The invariant named 'CompleteOCLInvariantFail' in the context of Detritus class will always fail, stating a failure along with the class type name.

specification: Each action in a Mixin must have a unique term.
The color of a fruit should not be black.
The name of a fruit should not be 'Foo'.
A fruit in the OclTest package must not be pink in color.

ocl: context Apple inv: label.oclIsUndefined() implies true
specification: If the label of an apple is undefined, then the condition is considered as true.

ocl: context Color inv: self <> Color::black implies true
specification: In the context of the Color class, if the color is not black, then the condition is always true.
The name of the variable must not be null.
An EOperationQualifier instance must not have any parameters in its operation.
The type of a TypeVariable must not be abstract.
The containment property of the parent composite reference of a CREATE operation must be true.

ocl: context CREATE inv: let variableReference : VariableReference = self.parent.oclAsType(VariableReference), queryResult : EObjectReference = variableReference.referencedVariable.oclAsType(QueryVariable).queryResult.oclAsType(EObjectReference) in if self.parent.oclIsKindOf(EObjectReference) then self.parent.oclAsType(EObjectReference).elements->size() = 1 else if self.parent.oclIsKindOf(VariableReference) then if variableReference.referencedVariable.oclIsKindOf(QueryVariable) then if variableReference.referencedVariable.oclAsType(QueryVariable).queryResult.oclIsKindOf(EObjectReference) then queryResult.elements->size() = 1 else false endif else true endif else true endif endif
specification: The 'CREATE' context must ensure the unique parental relationship based on the following conditional structure: If the parent is an instance of EObjectReference, then it should contain exactly one element. Conversely, if the parent is an instance of VariableReference and the referenced variable is a QueryVariable whose result is an EObjectReference, then the result should also contain exactly one element. In all other circumstances not explicitly defined, the conditions should default to true.

ocl: context SET inv: let variableReference : VariableReference = self.referenceOwner.oclAsType(VariableReference), queryResult : EObjectReference = variableReference.referencedVariable.oclAsType(QueryVariable).queryResult.oclAsType(EObjectReference) in if self.referenceOwner.oclIsKindOf(EObjectReference) then self.referenceOwner.oclAsType(EObjectReference).elements->size() = 1 else if self.referenceOwner.oclIsKindOf(VariableReference) then if variableReference.referencedVariable.oclIsKindOf(QueryVariable) then if variableReference.referencedVariable.oclAsType(QueryVariable).queryResult.oclIsKindOf(EObjectReference) then queryResult.elements->size() = 1 else false endif else true endif else true endif endif
specification: In the context of a SET, it should have a unique parent. This is validated by checking that if the reference owner of the set is an EObjectReference, it should contain exactly one element. Otherwise, if the reference owner is a VariableReference associated with a QueryVariable, whose query result is also an EObjectReference, then this result should also have exactly one element. In all other scenarios, the condition should automatically be satisfied.

ocl: context MOVE inv: let variableReference : VariableReference = self.newParent.oclAsType(VariableReference), queryResult : EObjectReference = variableReference.referencedVariable.oclAsType(QueryVariable).queryResult.oclAsType(EObjectReference) in if self.newParent.oclIsKindOf(EObjectReference) then self.newParent.oclAsType(EObjectReference).elements->size() = 1 else if self.newParent.oclIsKindOf(VariableReference) then if variableReference.referencedVariable.oclIsKindOf(QueryVariable) then if variableReference.referencedVariable.oclAsType(QueryVariable).queryResult.oclIsKindOf(EObjectReference) then queryResult.elements->size() = 1 else false endif else true endif else true endif endif
specification: In the context of a MOVE operation, if the new parent is an instance of EObjectReference, it should contain exactly one element. If the new parent is a VariableReference pointing to a QueryVariable which results in an EObjectReference, then this EObjectReference should also contain exactly one element.

ocl: context User inv: self.Gender = Sex::male or self.Gender = Sex::female
specification: The gender of a user must be either male or female.
The name of a PetriNet must only contain alphabetic characters, digits, or underscores, and must start with an alphabetic character or an underscore.
The name of a place must start with a letter or underscore, followed by any combination of letters, digits, or underscores.
The number of tokens at a place should be zero or positive.
The name of a transition must match the pattern where it starts with a letter or underscore, followed by any number of letters, digits, or underscores.
The predecessor and successor of an arc must not be the same.

ocl: context Arc inv: (self.predecessor.oclIsKindOf(Place) and self.successor.oclIsKindOf(Transition) or (self.predecessor.oclIsKindOf(Transition) and self.successor.oclIsKindOf(Place)))
specification: An arc should connect either a place to a transition or a transition to a place, but not entities of the same type.
The weight of an arc must be greater than or equal to 1.

ocl: context Constraint inv: true -- _'context'.ownedRule->excluding(self)->forAll(name <> self.name or stereotype <> self.stereotype)
specification: In the context of a Constraint, it should have a unique combination of name and stereotype among all the rules owned by the same context, excluding itself.

ocl: context Operation inv: bodyExpression <> null and bodyExpression.oclAsType(ExpressionInOCL).ownedBody <> null implies CompatibleBody(bodyExpression)
specification: For any operation, if the body expression is not null and the owned body of the expression is not null, then the body must be compatible with the operation.
The implementation of the operation must be loadable.
Each operation must have a unique name for its preconditions.
Each postcondition owned by an operation must have a unique name.
The 'type' of a feature must not be invalid.
The type of a variable declaration must not be invalid.
The type of CollectionLiteralPart should not be invalid.
The type of a ShadowPart must not be invalid.
The type of a CallExp should not be invalid.
The type within an IfExp must not be invalid.
The type in a Let expression must not be invalid.
The type of a ShadowExp should not be invalid.
The type of the StateExp must not be invalid.
The type of a VariableExp should not be invalid.
Invariants within a class should have unique names.
The type of the body in a closure operation should conform to the iterator's type.
The type used by a SortedBy iterator must be comparable.
The type of a Boolean literal expression must be Boolean.
The kind of a collection literal expression must not be of the abstract type 'Collection'.

ocl: context CollectionLiteralExp inv: kind = CollectionKind::Set implies type.oclIsKindOf(SetType)
specification: If the kind of a CollectionLiteralExp is a Set, then its type must be a SetType.

ocl: context CollectionLiteralExp inv: kind = CollectionKind::OrderedSet implies type.oclIsKindOf(OrderedSetType)
specification: If the kind of a CollectionLiteralExp is an OrderedSet, then its type must be of the kind OrderedSetType.

ocl: context CollectionLiteralExp inv: kind = CollectionKind::Sequence implies type.oclIsKindOf(SequenceType)
specification: If the collection kind is a sequence, then the type of the collection must be a kind of Sequence Type.

ocl: context CollectionLiteralExp inv: kind = CollectionKind::Bag implies type.oclIsKindOf(BagType)
specification: If the kind of a collection literal expression is defined as a Bag, then the type of the collection must be of BagType.
The type of a collection item must match the type of the item it owns.
The type of an EnumLiteralExp must be the same as the owning enumeration of the referred literal.
The type of the owned condition in an IfExp must be Boolean.
The type of an IntegerLiteralExp instance must be Integer.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: name = 'any' implies ownedIterators->size() = 1
specification: The operation named 'any' must have exactly one iterator.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: name = 'any' implies type = ownedSource.type.oclAsType(CollectionType).elementType
specification: In the context of IteratorExp, if the name is 'any', then the type should be the same as the element type of the collection type of the owned source.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: name = 'any' implies ownedBody.type = 'Boolean'
specification: If the name of an Iterator Expression is 'any', then the type of the owned body must be 'Boolean'.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: name = 'closure' implies ownedIterators->size() = 1
specification: If the name of the expression is 'closure', then it must have exactly one owned iterator.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: name = 'closure' implies if ownedSource.type.oclIsKindOf(SequenceType) or ownedSource.type.oclIsKindOf(OrderedSetType) then type.oclIsKindOf(OrderedSetType) else type.oclIsKindOf(SetType) endif
specification: For an Iterator Expression named 'closure', if the type of the owned source is either a SequenceType or an OrderedSetType, then its type should be an OrderedSetType. Otherwise, the type should be a SetType.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: name = 'closure' implies ownedSource.type.oclAsType(CollectionType).elementType = if ownedBody.type.oclIsKindOf(CollectionType) then ownedBody.type.oclAsType(CollectionType).elementType else ownedBody.type endif
specification: If the name of an IteratorExp is 'closure', then the type of the element in the source collection must match the type of the body element. If the body type is a collection, then it must match the element type of the body's collection type; otherwise, it should match the body type directly.
For an Iterator Expression, if its name is 'closure', then the element type of its collection type must be the same as the element type of the source's collection type.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: name = 'collect' implies if ownedSource.type.oclIsKindOf(SequenceType) or ownedSource.type.oclIsKindOf(OrderedSetType) then type.oclIsKindOf(SequenceType) else type.oclIsKindOf(BagType) endif
specification: If the name of an iterator expression is 'collect', then the type of the collected elements depends on the type of the owned source. Specifically, if the owned source is of type SequenceType or OrderedSetType, the type of the iterator expression should be SequenceType. If the owned source is of any other type, the type of the iterator expression should be BagType.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: name = 'collect' implies type.oclAsType(CollectionType).elementType = ownedBody.type.flattenedType()
specification: For an Iterator Expression named 'collect', the element type of the collection must be equal to the flattened type of the body that is owned by the expression.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: name = 'sortedBy' implies if ownedSource.type.oclIsKindOf(SequenceType) or ownedSource.type.oclIsKindOf(BagType) then type.oclIsKindOf(SequenceType) else type.oclIsKindOf(OrderedSetType) endif
specification: For an IteratorExp, if the name is 'sortedBy', then if the type of the owned source is either SequenceType or BagType, the type should be SequenceType; otherwise, the type should be OrderedSetType.
For an IteratorExp named 'sortedBy', the element type of the collection that is the result of the sorted operation must be the same as the element type of the collection on which it is invoked.
The type of IterateExp should be equal to the type of its owned result.
The 'ownedResult' of an IterateExp should have exactly one 'ownedInit'.
The type of the LetExp must be equal to the type owned within it.
The 'ownedSource' of a 'LoopExp' should be of or a subtype of 'CollectionType'.
In the LoopExp context, for all owned iterators, if they exist, they must not have any initializers.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: isSafe implies not ownedSource.type.oclAsType(CollectionType).isNullFree
specification: If an IteratorExp is marked as safe, then its owned source, if not null and of type CollectionType, must not be configured to allow null values.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: isSafe implies ownedIterators->forAll(isRequired)
specification: If an IteratorExpression is marked as safe, then all its owned iterators must be required.

ocl: context IteratorExp inv: (not isSafe and ownedIterators->exists(isRequired)) implies ownedSource.type.oclAsType(CollectionType).isNullFree
specification: If an IteratorExp is not safe and has any owned iterator that is required, then the type of its owned source must be a collection type that can not be null.

ocl: context IterateExp inv: isSafe implies not ownedSource.type.oclAsType(CollectionType).isNullFree
specification: If the 'IterateExp' is marked as safe, then its owned source, if non-null and a collection type, must not be free of nulls.

ocl: context IterateExp inv: isSafe implies ownedIterators->forAll(isRequired)
specification: If an IterateExp is marked as safe, then all its owned iterators are required.

ocl: context IterateExp inv: (not isSafe and ownedIterators->exists(isRequired)) implies ownedSource.type.oclAsType(CollectionType).isNullFree
specification: If an IterateExp is not safe and has any owned iterator that is required, then the type of its owned source must be a non-null collection.
A MessageExp must either have exactly one owned called operation or exactly one owned sent signal, but not both.
The target of a MessageExp should not be a collection type.
The number of arguments in an operation call must match the number of parameters defined in the referenced operation.

ocl: context OperationCallExp inv: (ownedSource <> null) and isSafe implies not ownedSource.isRequired
specification: For an OperationCallExp, if the owned source is not null and the operation is marked as safe, then it is implied that the owned source is not required.

ocl: context Conveyor inv: Conveyor.allInstances()->forAll(var | var.piece->size()<=var.capacity) and Piece.allInstances()->forAll(z| z.conveyor->size() =1)
specification: Every conveyor must have a piece count that does not exceed its capacity, and each piece must belong to exactly one conveyor.

ocl: context University inv: self.enrollments->collect(x | x.student)->asSet() ->forAll(s: Student | s.enrollments->intersection(self.enrollments)->size()=1)
specification: Each student should be enrolled in any given enrollment of a university exactly once.

ocl: context University inv: self.enrollments->isUnique(x: Enrollment | x.id)
specification: Each enrollment in a university must have a unique ID.

ocl: context Student inv: Adult.allInstances()->notEmpty() and Child.allInstances()->isEmpty() and Teenager.allInstances()->isEmpty() and (self.oclIsKindOf(Adult) implies self.universities->size() = 3)
specification: There must be at least one instance of an Adult. There should be no instances of Child or Teenager. If the student is an Adult, they should be enrolled in exactly three universities.

ocl: context University inv: self.students->forAll( x:Student | not x.oclIsKindOf(Child) )
specification: No student at the university should be a child.
A company must ensure that all members of its projects are its employees.

ocl: context Worker inv: not ( projects->select(p|p.status = ProjectStatus::active)->select(p|p.size=ProjectSize::big)->size() * 2 + projects->select(p|p.status = ProjectStatus::active)->select(p|p.size=ProjectSize::medium)->size() > 3)
specification: A worker is not considered overloaded if the total composed of twice the number of large active projects plus the number of medium active projects does not exceed three.

ocl: context Project inv: status = ProjectStatus::active implies (requirements->select(q|not members->exists(m | m.qualifications->includes(q))))->isEmpty()
specification: For an active project, all required qualifications must be met by the qualifications of at least one member of the project.
A worker should not be overloaded such that the sum of twice the number of big active projects plus the number of medium active projects exceeds three.
A property assignment's value attribute should not reference variables with types across multiple domains.
The number of tokens in a place should be non-negative.
The source and the destination of an arc must be different.
The number of tokens to be consumed on an arc should be positive.
A rear-end collision must involve exactly two vehicles.
A traveling vehicle should have exactly one traveler who is a driver.
A road traffic accident should involve exactly one vehicle.

ocl: context CrashedVehicle inv: self.travel.travelers->forAll(t|t.oclIsKindOf(Victim) and t.oclAsType(Victim).accident = self.accident)
specification: For all travelers in a crashed vehicle, each traveler must be a victim of the accident associated with that vehicle.
